original_hypothesis.title: Artist Can Retroactively Soulbind NFTs to Prevent Secondary Market Sales
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `soulBounded` status is checked dynamically during `safeTransferFrom`. The artist can change this status at any time via `updateArtSettings`, retroactively locking previously minted and transferable NFTs.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Users may purchase an NFT on the primary market with the expectation of transferability. The artist can rug-pull this expectation by flipping the soulbound switch, destroying the asset's liquidity.

proof_of_signal.pattern: soulBounded(ids_[i]) is queried dynamically during transfer rather than being immutable per token

preconditions: Art is initially created with soulBounded = false

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Dynamic state check in a core ERC1155 transfer hook.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Artist kills the secondary market to force users to buy new editions directly from them, or simply to grief holders.

economic_questions: Is there any timelock or warning before soulbound status changes?
Can this be used to extort holders?
validation_requirements: Attempt to transfer and verify it reverts

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Redirection of secondary volume to primary sales

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire market cap of the collection

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if soulBounded can be toggled back and forth (it can)

validation_result.impact_summary: Artist can retroactively lock all previously-minted transferable NFTs by toggling soulBounded to true via updateArtSettings, destroying secondary market liquidity for all holders.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The soulBounded status for PhiNFT1155 tokens is not stored per-token at mint time. Instead, it is queried dynamically from PhiFactory's arts storage during every transfer. The artist (art creator) can call PhiFactory.updateArtSettings at any time to flip soulBounded from false to true. There is no timelock, no governance check, and no restriction preventing this change after tokens have been minted. This means users who minted tokens expecting transferability can have their tokens retroactively locked by the artist with a single transaction.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Three code locations conclusively prove the defect: (1) PhiNFT1155.soulBounded() dynamically reads from PhiFactory, (2) safeTransferFrom and safeBatchTransferFrom call soulBounded() at transfer time and revert if true, (3) PhiFactory.updateArtSettings allows the artist to set soulBounded_ to any value with no restriction based on numberMinted or any timelock.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Reads soulBounded status dynamically from PhiFactory storage rather than storing it immutably per-token at mint time. Any change to arts[artId].soulBounded in PhiFactory instantly affects all existing tokens.
Checks soulBounded dynamically at transfer time. If the artist has toggled soulBounded to true after minting, all transfers revert with TokenNotTransferable.
Same dynamic soulBounded check applied to batch transfers. All tokens in the batch become non-transferable when soulBounded is toggled.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
function updateArtSettings(
    uint256 artId_,
    string memory url_,
    address receiver_,
    uint256 maxSupply_,
    uint256 mintFee_,
    uint256 startTime_,
    uint256 endTime_,
    bool soulBounded_,
    IPhiNFT1155Ownable.RoyaltyConfiguration memory configuration
)
    external
    onlyArtCreator(artId_)
{
    ...
    art.soulBounded = soulBounded_;
    ...
}
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Artist can set soulBounded to true at any time, with no check on whether tokens have already been minted (numberMinted > 0), no timelock, and no governance approval required.

calling_sequence: 2. onlyArtCreator modifier passes since caller is arts[artId].artist
4. Any subsequent transfer attempt calls PhiNFT1155.safeTransferFrom or safeBatchTransferFrom
6. artData returns arts[artId] which now has soulBounded=true
7. The condition (from_ != address(0) && soulBounded(id_)) is true, causing revert TokenNotTransferable
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The complete path from artist action to holder impact: PhiFactory.updateArtSettings sets arts[artId].soulBounded = true. PhiNFT1155.soulBounded(tokenId) reads phiFactoryContract.artData(_tokenIdToArtId[tokenId]).soulBounded, which returns the updated true value. PhiNFT1155.safeTransferFrom checks `if (from_ != address(0) && soulBounded(id_)) revert TokenNotTransferable()`. Since from_ is not address(0) for normal transfers, the transfer reverts. No timelock or immutability check exists anywhere in this path.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration affects this vulnerability. The defect is inherent in the contract logic.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: 1. Artist creates art with soulBounded=false via PhiFactory.createArt. 2. Users mint NFTs via PhiFactory.claim, paying mint fees. 3. Users list NFTs on secondary markets or plan to transfer. 4. Artist calls PhiFactory.updateArtSettings(artId, ..., soulBounded=true, ...). Only gas cost required. 5. All transfers now revert with TokenNotTransferable. 6. Artist can toggle back to false at will, creating uncertainty and destroying market confidence. The attack is trivially executable, costs only gas, and is immediately effective.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH - All holders of the affected art's tokens lose transferability instantly. NFTs listed on secondary markets become unliquidatable. The artist can use this as leverage to extort holders, manipulate market perception, or grief the community. The bidirectional toggle (can be set back to false) adds an additional manipulation vector. This violates user expectations established at mint time and constitutes a rug-pull capability.
recommended_mitigations: Make soulBounded immutable after the first token is minted: add `if (art.numberMinted > 0 && art.soulBounded != soulBounded_) revert CannotChangeSoulboundAfterMint();` in updateArtSettings
Store soulBounded status per-token at mint time rather than reading dynamically from factory
Add a timelock mechanism for soulBounded changes with a grace period for holders to exit
Only allow soulBounded to transition from false->true (never back) if it must be changeable, with advance notice via events
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy PhiFactory and PhiNFT1155. 2. Create art with soulBounded=false. 3. Mint tokens to user address. 4. Verify safeTransferFrom succeeds. 5. Call updateArtSettings with soulBounded=true. 6. Attempt safeTransferFrom and verify it reverts with TokenNotTransferable. 7. Call updateArtSettings with soulBounded=false. 8. Verify safeTransferFrom succeeds again, demonstrating the toggle.
uncertainties: Whether the protocol considers this an intentional design choice giving artists full control, though no documentation suggests this is intended behavior
additional_testing_needed: Verify the complete toggle cycle (false->true->false) works as described
Test interaction with marketplace approvals - do approved operators also get blocked
Test whether the owner (PhiFactory) can override the artist's soulbound setting
